avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations are numerous. As a consequence of new snow and wind, deep wind slabs formed. Natural avalanches are further possible. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation they can in isolated cases reach very large size and in some places endanger transportation routes situated at relatively high altitudes.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack in all aspects.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
The wind will be moderate to strong. Continuous warming to 2600 m.
We recommend that you consult the most recent avalanche bulletin.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Wind-blown snow: the probability of hikers triggering avalanches remains high, while the danger of spontaneous avalanches is slightly decreasing.
Mild temperatures and foehn winds: spontaneous dry and wet avalanches will increase already during the morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northwesterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. High-altitude starting zones have been replenished. As a consequence of the wind the wind slabs will increase in size once again until Monday. They can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally,. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches easily, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

With the freezing level at 2800 metres combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, wet and moist avalanches are expected from the morning onwards, some of which may be large in isolated areas. On steep sunny slopes the danger will increase rapidly.
The conditions are dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities. Careful route selection is required.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, and up to 20 cm in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2000 m, especially along the border with France.
Until Monday the wind will be strong over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches have been released in the last few days. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people in the last two days.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow are to be assessed with care and prudence. In particular, however, the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from the early morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant and reach large size in isolated cases.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and the danger is greater.

Significant warming: On steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches will increase during the day. In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can as before reach fairly large size.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached in the course of the day probably, but already the late morning at low and intermediate altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. In some regions 70 to 120 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1800 m.
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar in some places above the tree line. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.
Sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. The danger of dry and moist avalanches will increase a little during the day. In these regions, avalanche activity has already peaked.

bulletin.highlights: Even single freeriders can release avalanches very easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line, caution is to be exercised, including in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of the strong to storm force foehn wind from the north more natural avalanches are possible. This applies in case of releases originating from very steep leeward starting zones, in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet small and medium sized avalanches are possible. This applies especially on very steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Saturday. Thus far the wind has been strong to storm force.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Avalanches can be released very easily. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.

bulletin.highlights: Even single freeriders can release avalanches very easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line, caution is to be exercised, including in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of the strong to storm force foehn wind from the north more natural avalanches are possible. This applies in case of releases originating from very steep leeward starting zones, in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet small and medium sized avalanches are possible. This applies especially on very steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Saturday. Thus far the wind has been strong to storm force. Some snow will fall. This applies in particular in the north.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

bulletin.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for very extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. A high (level 4) danger of natural avalanches exists.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh and older wind slabs are to be assessed with care and prudence. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of last week can be released even by a single winter sport participant. In gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and above the tree line slab avalanches are possible, even large ones in isolated cases.

During the day: In steep rocky terrain and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The foehn wind has transported a lot of snow.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.
In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Moist and wet avalanches are possible from the morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong to storm force northerly foehn wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of avalanches being released will increase.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
With the freezing level at 2800 metres combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, wet and moist avalanches are expected from the morning onwards, including large ones in isolated areas.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell in the last few days above approximately 2000 m.
Until Monday the wind will be moderate to strong. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Isolated medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people on Saturday.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. In addition the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from the early morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence above the tree line. These can be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size.

Significant warming to 2700 m: On steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached in the course of the day probably, but already the late morning at low and intermediate altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. The strong wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh and older wind slabs are to be assessed with care and prudence. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence above the tree line. These can as before be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size.

Significant warming: On steep sunny slopes and at the base of rock walls the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches will increase during the day. Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes also at low and intermediate altitudes.

An increasing number of medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Thursday in all altitude zones.
The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of solar radiation.

bulletin.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. Above the tree line avalanche prone wind slabs formed, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep sunny slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released easily or naturally in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist avalanches. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind will be strong over a wide area. Over a wide area large surface-area wind slabs will form. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are easy to recognise. Experience and restraint are required. As the day progresses as a consequence of solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of moist avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of Thursday is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. In particular above approximately 1900 m sometimes large wind slabs formed. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2300 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow fell on Saturday. The wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow fell on Saturday. The wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and wind. Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.
avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger. Slab avalanches are still possible. Dry and moist avalanches on northeast, north and northwest facing slopes. Moist and wet avalanches on west, south and east facing slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche prone locations for moist avalanches are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes. The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on steep shady slopes. Gullies and bowls are especially unfavourable. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
